name: Lily Tomlin
bio: Lily Tomlin is an American comedian and actress who began in stand-up and sketch comedy before a long screen career. Her breakout came on Rowan and Martins Laugh-In (1969-1973), where she created enduring characters including telephone operator Ernestine and the precocious child Edith Ann. She won a Grammy for the 1971 album This Is a Recording, the first solo woman to win the comedy category, and later earned Tony Awards, multiple Emmys, and an Academy Award nomination.

timeline.ev: Joins Rowan and Martins Laugh-In, introduces Ernestine and Edith Ann
Wins Grammy for the comedy album This Is a Recording
Broadway solo debut Appearing Nitely earns a Special Tony Award
The Search for Signs of Intelligent Life in the Universe opens on Broadway
Receives the Mark Twain Prize for American Humor

accolades: First solo woman to win a Grammy for Best Comedy Recording (1972)
Mark Twain Prize for American Humor (2003)
